JavaScript tests for converting various data types to numbers, including strings, objects, and errors.


## Files
- **[15.07.01-001.js](15.07.01-001.js.md)**: A test to verify that converting the string "123456" to a number results in 123456.
- **[15.07.01-002.js](15.07.01-002.js.md)**: Tests if converting the string "123456" to a number results in a type of "number".
- **[15.07.01-003.js](15.07.01-003.js.md)**: Tests if converting a new Object to a Number results in a "number" type.
- **[15.07.01-004.js](15.07.01-004.js.md)**: Tests if converting the number 753 to a Number object equals 753.
- **[15.07.01-005.js](15.07.01-005.js.md)**: Tests that calling `Number()` returns positive zero.
- **[15.07.01-006.js](15.07.01-006.js.md)**: Tests if converting an Error object to a Number results in NaN.
- **[15.07.01-007.js](15.07.01-007.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of the `Number` function with a non-numeric string input.
- **[15.07.01-008.js](15.07.01-008.js.md)**: Tests if converting the string "abcdefg" to a number results in NaN.
- **[15.07.01-009.js](15.07.01-009.js.md)**: Tests if converting a function returning Infinity to a number results in NaN.
- **[15.07.01-010.js](15.07.01-010.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of the Number object when reassigned to null and then used.
